title: Ops runbook + review cadence (law-change -> re-verify -> re-sign-off) + outcome tracking + maintenance rotation
context: Law changes constantly, and static legal content is a liability if not maintained. After the first successful handoff (task 018), the project must establish sustainable operations: a named maintenance rotation, an enforced review cadence (law-change triggers re-verification and attorney re-sign-off before content returns to service), outcome tracking without PII, and a gated process for expanding to additional domains or jurisdictions. Depends on task 018.
objective: Produce the operations runbook and governance documents for sustainable post-delivery maintenance of Know-Your-Rights: the maintenance rotation, the law-change -> re-verify -> attorney re-sign-off workflow, the outcome tracking format (no PII, no vanity metrics), link and staleness monitoring procedures, and the gated attorney-reviewed process for adding a second domain or jurisdiction.
acceptanceCriteria: Ops runbook covers: publish and deploy procedures, link-check and staleness monitoring cadence, source re-verification workflow when law changes, partner support process, and escalation path for disputed content

Review cadence documented and enforced: a material law change (new statute, amended rule, controlling case decision) triggers re-verification of affected content and attorney re-sign-off before the content returns to normal service; the lastVerified/validUntil fail-safe (task 007) enforces this at runtime
Named maintenance rotation documented: at least two named individuals or roles responsible for monitoring, re-verification queues, and partner communication

Outcome tracking format defined: records content distributed (aggregate), documented beneficiary use count, neutrality/currency audit status, and date — zero reader PII, zero engagement vanity metrics

Expansion process documented: adding a second domain (worker rights) or jurisdiction requires a new source-vetting task, a licensed attorney admitted in that jurisdiction, and an editorial review before any new HIGH-tier content ships; process is attorney-gated
Runbook reviewed by maintainer and steward; maintenance rotation members named and consented
output: A governance document set (docs/ops-runbook.md, docs/review-cadence.md, docs/expansion-process.md) covering sustainable post-delivery maintenance: rotation, law-change re-verification workflow, outcome tracking format, and the gated expansion process for new domains/jurisdictions.
